summary: web3-secrets-detector exfiltrates host info and environment to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: The package contains an install-time exfiltration payload in package.json (line 30) that requires child_process, os, and https, gathers host identifiers (hostname, cwd, process.env) and POSTs them to https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js extends this behavior: it requires fs/os/https/child_process, runs execSync('whoami'), reads os.hostname(), uses fs.existsSync to enumerate filesystem paths, and issues https.request POSTs plus a curl invocation pointing at the same hardcoded https://ddjidd564.github.io endpoint (lines 142-143, 161, 233-234, 324). The destination is an anonymous GitHub Pages site unrelated to any documented publisher and has no relation to the package's stated purpose; there is no configuration override, no benign data path. Installing or loading this package causes the installer's host metadata, working directory, environment variables, and shell command output to be transmitted to an attacker-controlled host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
